learningObjectives: Use core public places and directions vocabulary at A2 level.
Use imperatives to give and follow clear directions.
Use through, across and along to describe movement routes.
Read short signs and messages about public places.
Listen to longer extracts and identify route details.
Write short directions to meet at a landmark.
Answer interview questions about places in town.
grammarFocus: Prepositions of movement: through, across, along
unit.readingSkillFocus: Multiple Choice with Short Texts (Signs, Messages)

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Pedestrians must use the pavement near the crossing.

vocabularyBank.vietnameseMeaning: lối qua đường / vạch sang đường
vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Wait at the crossing until the light turns green.
The war monument stands in the main square.
The museum entrance is on River Street.
Follow the signs to the nearest exit.
We meet friends in the town square every Saturday.
There is a famous statue in front of the library.
The clock tower is a well-known landmark in our city.
Walk on the pavement, not on the road.
Cross the river on the footbridge near the park.
Turn left at the roundabout after the monument.
The bus stop is at the junction of Oak Road and Hill Street.

grammarReference.explanation: Use the base verb (without subject) to give clear instructions and directions. Add please to be polite. Use turn, go, walk, cross and follow for routes.
examples: Walk along the pavement to the crossing.
Go through the park and cross the footbridge.
commonMistakes: You turn left at the crossing (×) → Turn left at the crossing (✓)
Walk to along the square (×) → Walk along the square (✓)
Please crosses at the lights (×) → Please cross at the lights (✓)

grammarReference.structure: Prepositions of movement: through, across, along
grammarReference.explanation: Use through for movement inside a space, across for from one side to another, and along for following a line or path.
examples: Walk through the park to reach the square.
Go across the footbridge over the river.
Follow the path along the river to the monument.
commonMistakes: Walk across the park tunnel (×) → Walk through the park tunnel (✓)
Go along the road to the other side (×) → Go across the road to the other side (✓)
Walk through the pavement line (×) → Walk along the pavement (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Public Places and Directions Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve public places and directions words at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Places Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: A person walking in the street is a _____.
questions.explanation: Pedestrian (người đi bộ) đi bộ trên vỉa hè hoặc qua đường.

questions.questionText: The safe place where people walk over a road is a _____.
questions.explanation: Crossing (vạch sang đường) là nơi người đi bộ qua đường.

questions.questionText: A stone figure of a person or animal in a public place is a _____.
questions.explanation: Statue (tượng) là tác phẩm điêu khắc công cộng.

questions.questionText: A large open area in the centre of a town is a _____.
questions.explanation: Square (quảng trường) là không gian mở giữa thành phố.

questions.questionText: The door you use to go into a building is the _____.
questions.explanation: Entrance (lối vào) là cửa vào.

questions.questionText: A well-known building or place that helps you find your way is a _____.
questions.explanation: Landmark (địa danh) giúp định hướng trong thành phố.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the places words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với một nghĩa rõ ràng. Monument và landmark đều liên quan địa điểm nổi bật.

pairs.right: a structure built to remember an important person or event
the way out of a building or area
the path at the side of a road for walkers
a bridge for people to walk across

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: Drivers go around a circular island in the road at a _____.
questions.explanation: Roundabout (bùng binh) là nút giao hình tròn.

questions.questionText: Two roads meet at a _____.
questions.explanation: Junction (ngã tư) là nơi các đường giao nhau.

questions.questionText: Please walk on the _____ — do not walk on the road.
questions.explanation: Pavement (vỉa hè) dành cho người đi bộ.

questions.questionText: Tourists took photos of the war _____ in the square.
questions.explanation: Monument (đài tưởng niệm) tưởng nhớ sự kiện lịch sử.

questions.questionText: We crossed the river on a narrow _____.
questions.explanation: Footbridge (cầu bộ hành) chỉ dành cho người đi bộ.

exercises.title: Apply: Words in Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: Use the [0] on River Street. | Wait at the [1] for the green light. | Meet me in the town [2].
questions.explanation: entrance (lối vào); crossing (vạch sang đường); square (quảng trường).

content.template: Use the [0] on River Street. Wait at the [1] for the green light. Meet me in the town [2].

questions.questionText: Complete: The clock tower is a famous [0]. | Walk on the [1], not the road. | Leave by the side [2] to the square.
questions.explanation: landmark (địa danh); pavement (vỉa hè); exit (lối ra).

content.template: The clock tower is a famous [0]. Walk on the [1], not the road. Leave by the side [2] to the square.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Directions and Movement
lessons.learningObjective: Use imperatives and prepositions through, across and along to give and understand directions.

exercises.title: Learn: Imperatives and Prepositions
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word or phrase to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: _____ left at the roundabout.
questions.explanation: Imperative: Turn left — không cần chủ ngữ.

questions.questionText: Walk _____ the pavement to the crossing.
questions.explanation: Along + đường đi dài: along the pavement.

questions.questionText: Go _____ the park to reach the square quickly.
questions.explanation: Through + không gian bên trong: through the park.

questions.questionText: _____ the footbridge to the other side of the river.
questions.explanation: Cross / Go across — qua từ bên này sang bên kia.

questions.questionText: Pedestrians should use the crossing to go _____ the road.
questions.explanation: Across the road — sang phía bên kia đường.

questions.questionText: _____ straight ahead until you see the statue.
questions.explanation: Imperative: Walk straight ahead.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct word.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] along the river path. | Go [1] the museum entrance. | [2] at the junction.
questions.explanation: Walk (imperative); through (inside); Turn (imperative).

content.template: [0] along the river path. Go [1] the museum entrance. [2] at the junction.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] the footbridge carefully. | Follow the signs to the [1]. | Go [2] the road at the crossing.
questions.explanation: Cross (imperative); exit (noun); across (movement).

content.template: [0] the footbridge carefully. Follow the signs to the [1]. Go [2] the road at the crossing.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Which instruction is correct for pedestrians at a busy junction?
questions.explanation: Please cross at the pedestrian crossing — mệnh lệnh lịch sự.

choices.text: Please crossing at the pedestrian crossing.
Please cross at the pedestrian crossing.
Please crosses at the pedestrian crossing.

questions.questionText: The guide said: 'Walk _____ the gallery to the monument room.'
questions.explanation: Along the gallery — đi theo hành lang.

questions.questionText: To leave the station, you must go _____ the ticket hall to the main exit.
questions.explanation: Through the ticket hall — đi xuyên qua không gian bên trong.

questions.questionText: Which sentence gives a clear direction?
questions.explanation: Turn right at the landmark — cấu trúc mệnh lệnh rõ ràng.

choices.text: Turning right at the landmark near the square.
Turn right at the landmark near the square.
You turning right at the landmark near the square.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Walk / along / the pavement / to / the crossing.
questions.explanation: Walk along the pavement to the crossing. — imperative + along.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Go / through / the park / and / cross / the footbridge.
questions.explanation: Go through the park and cross the footbridge. — through + across route.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Turn / left / at / the roundabout / after / the monument.
questions.explanation: Turn left at the roundabout after the monument. — direction sequence.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Museum Visitor Sign
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short public sign and understand directions, details and vocabulary in context.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Museum Sign
exercises.instructions: Read the sign carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: City Museum — Visitor Information
passage.text:
WELCOME TO RIVERSIDE CITY MUSEUM

ENTRANCE: Main door on River Street (near the statue).
EXIT: Side door to the square — for pedestrians only.

VISITOR ROUTE
1. Enter through the main entrance.
2. Walk along the gallery to the monument room.
3. Cross the footbridge display area to the exit.

SAFETY
• Do not run on the pavement outside.
• Use the marked crossing on River Street.
• The nearest landmark for taxis is the clock tower at the junction.

OPEN: Tuesday–Sunday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
Closed Mondays.

Questions? Ask at the information desk by the entrance.
passage.imagePrompt: A printed museum information sign with entrance and exit arrows, a small map and safety icons near a city statue.

questions.questionText: Where is the main entrance?
questions.explanation: Main door on River Street near the statue.

choices.text: In the square by the exit
On River Street near the statue
At the junction by the clock tower

questions.questionText: Who may use the side exit to the square?
questions.explanation: Side door to the square — for pedestrians only.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Sign
exercises.instructions: Read the sign again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: City Museum — Visitor Information
passage.text:
WELCOME TO RIVERSIDE CITY MUSEUM

ENTRANCE: Main door on River Street (near the statue).
EXIT: Side door to the square — for pedestrians only.

VISITOR ROUTE
1. Enter through the main entrance.
2. Walk along the gallery to the monument room.
3. Cross the footbridge display area to the exit.

SAFETY
• Do not run on the pavement outside.
• Use the marked crossing on River Street.
• The nearest landmark for taxis is the clock tower at the junction.

OPEN: Tuesday–Sunday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
Closed Mondays.

Questions? Ask at the information desk by the entrance.
passage.imagePrompt: A printed museum information sign with entrance and exit arrows, a small map and safety icons near a city statue.

questions.questionText: In step 3 of the visitor route, visitors _____ the footbridge display area.
questions.explanation: Cross the footbridge display area — đi qua khu trưng bày cầu.

questions.questionText: Why does the sign mention the clock tower?
questions.explanation: Nearest landmark for taxis at the junction.

choices.text: It is a landmark for finding taxis

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the sign one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.title: City Museum — Visitor Information
passage.text:
WELCOME TO RIVERSIDE CITY MUSEUM

ENTRANCE: Main door on River Street (near the statue).
EXIT: Side door to the square — for pedestrians only.

VISITOR ROUTE
1. Enter through the main entrance.
2. Walk along the gallery to the monument room.
3. Cross the footbridge display area to the exit.

SAFETY
• Do not run on the pavement outside.
• Use the marked crossing on River Street.
• The nearest landmark for taxis is the clock tower at the junction.

OPEN: Tuesday–Sunday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
Closed Mondays.

Questions? Ask at the information desk by the entrance.
passage.imagePrompt: A printed museum information sign with entrance and exit arrows, a small map and safety icons near a city statue.

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of this sign?
questions.explanation: Welcome + entrance/exit + route + safety for visitors.

choices.text: To sell tickets for the town square
To guide visitors around the museum safely

questions.questionText: Put the visitor route steps in order: (1) Monument room (2) Main entrance (3) Exit area
questions.explanation: Enter → gallery/monument → cross to exit.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Sign
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each item to the correct fact.

passage.title: City Museum — Visitor Information
passage.text:
WELCOME TO RIVERSIDE CITY MUSEUM

ENTRANCE: Main door on River Street (near the statue).
EXIT: Side door to the square — for pedestrians only.

VISITOR ROUTE
1. Enter through the main entrance.
2. Walk along the gallery to the monument room.
3. Cross the footbridge display area to the exit.

SAFETY
• Do not run on the pavement outside.
• Use the marked crossing on River Street.
• The nearest landmark for taxis is the clock tower at the junction.

OPEN: Tuesday–Sunday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
Closed Mondays.

Questions? Ask at the information desk by the entrance.
passage.imagePrompt: A printed museum information sign with entrance and exit arrows, a small map and safety icons near a city statue.

questions.questionText: Match each topic to a fact from the sign.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với thông tin cụ thể trên bảng hướng dẫn bảo tàng.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Tours and Station Directions
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to longer extracts about routes and identify landmarks, directions and movement details.

exercises.title: Learn: Walking Tour
exercises.instructions: Listen to the tour guide. Answer the first two questions.

script.title: Walking Tour Directions
script.setting: City square with a tour guide and tourists

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Welcome everyone! We're standing in the main square next to the famous statue.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Please walk along the pavement on your left. Go through the small park.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: At the junction, turn right. Cross the footbridge over the river.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Is the monument on this side of the river?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Yes. After the bridge, the war monument is straight ahead — it's an important landmark.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We'll use the pedestrian crossing to return. The museum entrance is near the clock tower.

script.audioNotes: Clear guide voice outdoors, light background ambience. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: Where does the tour start?
questions.explanation: Standing in the main square next to the famous statue.

choices.text: In the main square by the statue

questions.questionText: What is the first direction Elena gives?
questions.explanation: Walk along the pavement on your left.

choices.text: Walk along the pavement on the left

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Walking Tour
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same tour. Answer the next questions.

script.title: Walking Tour Directions
script.setting: City square with a tour guide and tourists

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Welcome everyone! We're standing in the main square next to the famous statue.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Please walk along the pavement on your left. Go through the small park.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: At the junction, turn right. Cross the footbridge over the river.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Is the monument on this side of the river?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Yes. After the bridge, the war monument is straight ahead — it's an important landmark.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We'll use the pedestrian crossing to return. The museum entrance is near the clock tower.

script.audioNotes: Clear guide voice outdoors, light background ambience. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: What do tourists do at the junction?
questions.explanation: At the junction, turn right.

questions.questionText: What landmark is straight ahead after the footbridge?
questions.explanation: The war monument is straight ahead.

choices.distractorNote: Mentioned at end, not after bridge

exercises.title: Check: Station to Square Directions
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new conversation. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Finding the Train Station Exit

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Excuse me, how do I get to the town square from here?

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.text: Leave through the main exit and turn left.

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.text: Walk along Station Road until you see the roundabout.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Do I cross the road at the junction?

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.text: No. Use the pedestrian crossing after the roundabout. Go across the road safely.

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.text: Walk through the park gate. The square with the statue is on your right.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Thank you! Is the clock tower a good landmark?

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.text: Yes, you can see it from the square.

script.audioNotes: Station announcements softly in background. Approx. 50 seconds.

questions.questionText: Where does Minh want to go?
questions.explanation: How do I get to the town square?

questions.questionText: How should Minh cross the road at the junction?
questions.explanation: Use the pedestrian crossing after the roundabout.

choices.text: Use the pedestrian crossing after the roundabout

questions.questionText: Which landmark can Minh see from the square?
questions.explanation: Yes, you can see the clock tower from the square.

choices.distractorNote: On the route, not visible from square

exercises.title: Apply: Match What You Heard
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the walking tour. Match each item to the correct detail.

script.title: Walking Tour Directions
script.setting: City square with a tour guide and tourists

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Welcome everyone! We're standing in the main square next to the famous statue.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Please walk along the pavement on your left. Go through the small park.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: At the junction, turn right. Cross the footbridge over the river.

lines.speaker: Elena

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We'll use the pedestrian crossing to return. The museum entrance is near the clock tower.

script.audioNotes: Clear guide voice outdoors, light background ambience. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: Match each item from the walking tour.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với hướng dẫn Elena đưa ra trong tour.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Write Directions
lessons.learningObjective: Write short directions to meet at a landmark using imperatives and movement prepositions.

exercises.title: Learn: Direction Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] left at the roundabout. | Walk [1] the pavement to the [2]. | [3] the footbridge to the square.
questions.explanation: Turn (imperative); along (preposition); crossing (noun); Cross (imperative).

content.template: [0] left at the roundabout. Walk [1] the pavement to the [2]. [3] the footbridge to the square.

exercises.title: Practice: Build Direction Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make correct direction sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Go / through / the park / to / the monument.
questions.explanation: Go through the park to the monument. — through + destination.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Walk / along / River Street / to / the museum / entrance.
questions.explanation: Walk along River Street to the museum entrance. — along + route.

exercises.title: Check: Directions to a Landmark
exercises.instructions: Write short directions. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: Your friend wants to meet you at the clock tower landmark in the town square on Saturday. Write a short message with directions from the train station exit.
prompts: Say where to start (train station exit).
Use at least two imperatives (e.g. turn, walk, cross).
Use through, across or along at least once.
Mention a landmark or public place from the unit.
successCriteria: Clear route from station to meeting place
Mentions a unit vocabulary place word

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Sam,

Leave the station exit and turn left. Walk along Station Road to the roundabout. Go across the road at the pedestrian crossing. Walk through the park gate. The square with the statue is on your right. The clock tower landmark is straight ahead.

See you,
Minh

vocabulary.criteria: Uses places words (square, crossing, landmark, entrance) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Directions are in a clear step-by-step order with greeting and closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Route from station to landmark is clear; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Direction Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the direction frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] the main entrance on River Street. | Walk [1] the gallery to the monument. | Use the side [2] to reach the square.
questions.explanation: Enter/Use (imperative context); along (path); exit (way out).

content.template: [0] the main entrance on River Street. Walk [1] the gallery to the monument. Use the side [2] to reach the square.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Places in Town
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about public places and giving directions using unit vocabulary and grammar.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Is there a famous landmark in your town? You say:
questions.explanation: Landmark + vị trí — câu trả lời mô tả địa danh.

choices.text: Yes, the pedestrian in the square is a famous landmark.
Yes, the clock tower in the square is a famous landmark.
Yes, I argue at the landmark every day.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How do pedestrians cross busy roads safely? You say:
questions.explanation: Use the crossing — an toàn cho người đi bộ.

choices.text: They use the monument and wait for the statue.
They use the pedestrian crossing and wait for the green light.
They use the exit and wait for the entrance.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Tell me how to get to the museum entrance. You say:
questions.explanation: Imperative + along — chỉ đường đơn giản.

choices.text: Walking along the pavement and turns left at the junction.
Walk along the pavement and turn left at the junction.
Walk along the pavement and turning left at the junction.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best phrase you would say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What can you see in the main square? You say:
questions.explanation: Statue + monument — mô tả quảng trường.

choices.text: There is a crossing and an exit near the pavement only.
choices.distractorNote: Road features not typical square focus

choices.text: There is a statue and an old monument near the fountain.
There is a pedestrian and a roundabout inside the museum.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How do you get to the other side of the river? You say:
questions.explanation: Cross the footbridge — across the river.

choices.text: I through the footbridge along the river.
I cross the footbridge near the park.
I along the footbridge across the monument.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Give directions to the nearest exit. You say:
questions.explanation: Go through + follow signs — chỉ lối ra.

choices.text: Go across the hall and follow the entrance signs on your left.
Go through the hall and follow the exit signs on your right.
Go along the exit and follow the roundabout on your right.

exercises.title: Check: Places and Directions Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about public places and directions. Speak for up to two minutes.

content.prompt: The examiner will ask you about landmarks, public places and how to give directions in your town.
content.pictureDescription: A city map showing a square with a statue, a footbridge over a river, a pedestrian crossing and a museum entrance.
followUpQuestions: What is a famous landmark in your area?
Where do pedestrians walk in busy streets?
How do you cross the road safely?
Is there a monument or statue near your home?
How do you get from the station to the town square?
Tell me how to find the museum entrance.
suggestedAnswers: The clock tower near the junction is a well-known landmark.
They walk on the pavement, not on the road.
I use the pedestrian crossing and wait for the green light.
Yes, there is a war monument in the main square.
Leave the exit, walk along Station Road and go through the park.
Walk along River Street and the entrance is near the statue.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Key words (pedestrian, crossing, landmark, entrance, footbridge) are understandable.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Responds with phrases or short sentences without long silences.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses imperatives or through/across/along in at least two answers.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses at least four different unit words correctly.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Responses
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each situation.

questions.questionText: A tourist asks how to reach the square from the footbridge. You say:
questions.explanation: Walk along + turn — chỉ đường sau cầu.

choices.text: Walk through the junction and turn along the monument.
Walk along the river path and turn right at the junction.
Walk across the statue and turn through the exit.

questions.questionText: Your friend is lost near the roundabout. You say:
questions.explanation: Landmark + imperative — định hướng bằng địa danh.

choices.text: Look for the pavement landmark. Cross at the entrance statue.
Look for the clock tower landmark. Cross at the pedestrian crossing.
Look for the exit landmark. Cross at the monument square.

questions.questionText: The examiner asks: How do tourists leave the museum to the square? You say:
questions.explanation: Side exit for pedestrians — khớp bài đọc.

choices.text: They use the main entrance for pedestrians to the junction.
They use the side exit for pedestrians to the square.
They use the roundabout for pedestrians to the statue.
